findings.evidence: grep/nl hit: line 54 defines `lemma mulAction_equivSubgroupOrbitsQuotientGroup_symm_mk`; line 94 uses it only to prove `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_symm_mk`.
findings.fix: Make this helper `private` or move it to an appropriate generic Mathlib-facing file only if it is intended as reusable generic API.
findings.issue: `mulAction_equivSubgroupOrbitsQuotientGroup_symm_mk` is a generic Mathlib-convention wrapper exported from the deck namespace, although the deck-specific public lemma at line 88 already exposes the needed representative rule.

findings.evidence: Existing parallel API is `[simp]` at `SubgroupFiberOrbit.lean:263` for `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquiv_mapOfLE`; this file's general lemmas at lines 249 and 286 have no attribute, while only representative lemmas at lines 273 and 302 are `[simp]`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `subgroupFiberOrbitQuotientEquivQuotientGroup_mapOfLE` and its regular-cover analogue at line 286; the representative specializations can then be kept only if they add value.
findings.issue: The characteristic inclusion-compatibility normal form is not tagged `[simp]`, so `simp` will not move an arbitrary `x : SubgroupFiberOrbitQuotient H b` across `Subgroup.quotientMapOfLE` and the deck-coset equivalence.

summary: The deck quotient equivalence has the main representative and inclusion API, but the public surface still exports an implementation helper and leaves the general inclusion normal form out of simp.
